fault_hypothesis: The overlap condition on line 61 uses strict inequalities (`b <= s` and `a >= e`), which treats adjacent/touching windows (where b==s or a==e) as non-conflicting. It should use `b < s` and `a > e` (or equivalently `not (b < s or a > e)`) to also reject touching boundaries.

spec.expr: all((b__1 < s or a__1 > e) == (not (b__1 <= s or a__1 >= e)) for s, e in timeline) == False or len(timeline) == 0

constraints.intent: Detect that the strict-vs-non-strict boundary check differs when window end equals an existing start or window start equals an existing end.

spec.expr: not any((b__1 == s or a__1 == e) and not (not (b__1 <= s or a__1 >= e)) for s, e in timeline)

constraints.intent: Check that touching-boundary cases (b==s or a==e) are correctly identified as conflicts by the overlap expression.

spec.expr: all((b__1 < s or a__1 > e) for s, e in timeline) or any(not (b__1 < s or a__1 > e) for s, e in timeline)

constraints.intent: Verify that using strict inequalities (< and >) would correctly flag adjacent-touching windows as conflicts.

spec.expr: not (__cbfl_result[0] == True and any(b == s or a == e for s, e in timeline for a, b in [window]))

constraints.intent: Ensure that a booking touching an existing boundary is never accepted (returned True).
